text.title: ai-in-project-management Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for ai-in-project-management. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Every AI-assisted PM decision must be logged with the format: "AI suggested [X] based on [data]; human PM approved/modified/rejected because [reason]."
rule.rationale: PMBOK 8 AI appendix requires human oversight and traceability for AI-assisted decisions; undocumented AI suggestions are not auditable and cannot demonstrate responsible AI use.

rule.statement: Agents must surface decisions for human approval rather than acting autonomously on PM actions (reassigning resources, closing issues, changing deadlines).
rule.rationale: PMBOK 8 explicitly requires human control for AI-assisted decisions; autonomous PM actions by agents without human approval violates the standard's ethical AI guidance.

rule.statement: Start AI integration with the three highest-value, lowest-risk use cases: status report generation, meeting summary to action items, overdue task alerts.
rule.rationale: These use cases have clear output, easy human verification, and no irreversible consequences — they build team confidence in AI PM tools before tackling riskier automation like resource reallocation.

rule.statement: Measure end-to-end flow time (idea to production), not just individual task velocity — the AI productivity paradox is invisible when you only measure individual output.
rule.rationale: DORA 2025: AI tools produce 21% more tasks and 98% more PRs at individual level, but organizational delivery metrics stay flat because bottlenecks shift to review, integration, and deployment. Individual velocity metrics create a false sense of acceleration.

rule.statement: ML risk prediction tools require 3–6 months of historical project data before predictions become reliable; do not deploy on novel project types without this baseline.
rule.rationale: Risk prediction models trained on insufficient or non-representative data produce confident-sounding but unreliable probability scores; this is more dangerous than no prediction because it suppresses human judgment.

rule.statement: Configure agents for decision support, not autonomous PM action: agents surface risk assessments, status summaries, and bottleneck alerts for human PM review — they do not reassign resources, close issues, or change deadlines unilaterally.
rule.rationale: PMBOK 8 requires human oversight for AI-assisted decisions; autonomous PM actions by agents without approval violates the ethical AI standard and removes accountability for outcomes.

rule.statement: Label all AI-generated risk scores as "AI-estimated" in output and audit log — require human PM validation before acting on them.
rule.rationale: AI risk assessments produce confident-sounding probability scores even when data is insufficient; the "AI-estimated" label prevents risk scores from being treated as validated measurements.

rule.statement: To detect the DORA AI productivity paradox: compare individual task completion rate vs. deployment frequency over the same period. If task rate rises while deployment frequency is flat, trace where work accumulates (review queue depth, staging backlog, deployment frequency).
rule.rationale: Bottleneck detection requires comparing two different metric types simultaneously; looking at only one metric produces the illusion of improvement while the real constraint stays invisible.

rule.statement: Agents cannot detect team morale, interpersonal conflicts, or burnout from project data — these are critical PM risk factors that only human observation can identify.
rule.rationale: Project health data (issue counts, velocity, cycle time) has no signal for human factors; treating data-driven AI health reports as complete PM risk assessments omits the most common causes of project failure.

rule.statement: Document every AI-assisted decision in the project log with the format: "AI suggested [X] based on [data]; human PM approved/modified/rejected because [reason]" — this is the PMBOK 8 traceability requirement.
rule.rationale: PMBOK 8 traceability requirement ensures human oversight is verifiable and allows auditors to confirm responsible AI use in project management.

rule.statement: Validate AI risk predictions against actual outcomes quarterly and retrain or adjust confidence levels based on prediction accuracy.
rule.rationale: Without validation feedback, AI models do not improve; quarterly review ensures predictions that miss by large margins are flagged and confidence scores are recalibrated or the model is replaced.
